title: Trump Administration Sees Striking Exodus of Legal Talent
summary: The departure of more than 10,000 federal lawyers has left some agencies without sufficient staff and has boosted the ranks of state attorneys general offices and advocacy groups.

title: Inside Trump’s Deal With the I.R.S. to Drop His $10 Billion Lawsuit
summary: Discussions among a group of lawyers with allegiance to the president were closely held. Some senior White House officials were said to have felt blindsided as the agreement took shape.

title: Trump Sends Tougher Peace Proposals Back to Iran, Officials Say
summary: His changes to the proposed deal were potentially designed to speed up the process by putting pressure on Iran to accept the current framework, one official said.

title: Israel Captures Crusader Castle That Symbolized Its Long Lebanon Occupation
summary: The seizure of Beaufort in southern Lebanon called up bitter memories in both countries amid a widening Israeli conflict with Hezbollah that seems far from over.

title: Trump Is Dominating GOP Primary Elections. Does That Mean Victory in the Midterms?
summary: Come November, the Republican Party will need the support of voters outside of President Trump’s base, many of whom are deeply dissatisfied with the economy and the Iran war.

title: After Voting Decision, a Month of Political Earthquakes Across the South
summary: As Republicans rush to redraw the region’s congressional maps, some voters are confused and concerned, and civil rights activists are gearing up for the fight of a generation.

title: He Was Satirized on British TV. Now He’s Trump’s Pick to Lead California.
summary: Steve Hilton grabbed headlines when he worked in conservative politics in Britain. His American political renaissance in the California governor’s race has bemused former British colleagues and rivals.

title: Platner’s Wife Flagged His Sexual Texts With Other Women as Maine Senate Race Began
summary: The wife of Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate in Maine, told the campaign he had sent sexual messages to other women.

title: In U.F.O. Files, Some Christians See Vexing Questions — and Demons
summary: The prospect of intelligent life elsewhere in the universe raises unsettling theological implications.

title: Tilly Norwood, A.I. Actress, Wants to Know Why Everyone’s Mad at Her
summary: The A.I. actress on her craft, the future of film and how she definitely does not intend to murder us.

title: The Museum of Wretched Ideas Has Reopened
summary: We seem to have forgotten more or less everything.

title: How to Make Americans Read Again
summary: The reading crisis is real, but the solution does not require new inventions.

title: Scene Stealer
summary: What happens when a reporter who has interviewed some of the biggest names in Hollywood sits down with the first A.I. actress?

title: Israel Day Parade to Return to New York Against a Changed Backdrop
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani did not attend the annual Israel Day Parade, which is taking place amid a deterioration of public support for the country and rising antisemitism across the United States.

title: Visiting Hours to Resume at NJ’s Delaney Hall ICE Detention Center After Protests
summary: Gov. Mikie Sherrill and other New Jersey elected officials have urged calm as demonstrators clashed with police while protesting conditions at the immigration detention center.

title: China’s Rise in Drug Development Looms Over U.S.
summary: Clinical trials in China are getting attention at an international oncology gathering in Chicago. China’s surging biotechnology industry is fueling alarm that U.S. dominance in the field is waning.

title: Who’s the Vegas Retiree With the Big Offer for Greenland?
summary: Clifford E. Stanley, a retired broker, explains his unusual mission, which caught a lot of people by surprise.
